request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We have 26 candidates. For each, we need to decide if it is valid: "useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge." We reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence. The allowed category_if_not values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other. Only use those if rejected; otherwise, category_if_not is null.

We'll go through each candidate. Since we have to output a JSON with verdicts.

First candidate: cand_038e0028c80228e1 - operational_sequence, title: "Supply Fan Operation in Unoccupied Mode", section_id: 5.22.4.1. Summary says: The supply fan runs whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode. Evidence quote: "The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode." This is specific operational knowledge from Guideline 36, section 5.22.4.1. It is useful and grounded. So it should be accepted. is_valid=true, reason: "Clear operational rule from Section 5.22.4.1", category_if_not: null.

Second: cand_6d698f1f58bc0128 - operational_sequence, title "Fan Speed Ramp Limitation", section 5.22.4.2. Summary: Fan speed changes are limited to no more than 10% per minute via a ramp function. Evidence: "Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute." This is also a clear operational rule, useful. Accept.

Third: cand_b3d8fb8678a42f70 - operational_sequence, title "Cooling Mode Fan Speed and SAT Control", section 5.22.4.4.c. Summary details reset logic for fan speed and SAT setpoint based on cooling loop signal. Evidence is a long quote that matches. This is core operational sequence. Accept.

Fourth: cand_f786609be89cc7be - fault_diagnostic_rule, title "Fan Maintenance Interval Alarm", section 5.22.5.1. Summary: Alarm when fan operated >1500 hours; reset on ack. Evidence: quote matches. This is a specific fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

Fifth: cand_e3b5bf4ea21be622 - fault_diagnostic_rule, title "Fan Status Mismatch Alarm", section 5.22.5.2. Summary: Alarm when fan status differs from command for 15 sec; Level 2 if commanded on but off, Level 4 if commanded off but on. Evidence matches, pages listed. Accept.

Sixth: cand_57112eabbe9649a2 - fault_diagnostic_rule, title "Filter Pressure Drop Alarm", section 5.22.5.3. Summary: Alarm when pressure drop exceeds larger of alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 min and fan speed >20% of MaxCoolSpeed. Alarm limit varies with fan speed: DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4. Evidence matches. Accept.

Seventh: cand_0598194188d67f7f - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Operating State Definition", section 5.22.6.2. Summary: Defines OS#1-4 based on valve commands as per Table 5.20.6.2. Evidence: "The OS of each FCU shall be defined... in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2." This is operational knowledge from AFDD section. Accept.

Eighth: cand_a995ec8f67d4f12f - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Required Points", section 5.22.6.3. Summary: Lists points: SAT, RAT, SATsp, HC, CC, FS. Evidence: "5.22.6.3. The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU:" It likely continues in the source, but the summary provides the list. It is operational (requirements for AFDD). Accept.

Ninth: cand_055e97fbd54ce73c - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Rolling Averages Calculation", section 5.22.6.4. Summary: Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of SAT, RAT, and number of OS changes in previous 60 minutes. Operator adjustable. Evidence: "5.22.6.4. The following values must be continuously calculated..." This is operational. Accept.

Tenth: cand_ae8e7d15a017232a - parameter_spec, title "AFDD Internal Variables Default Values", section 5.22.6.5. Summary: Lists internal variables with defaults: ΔTSF=0.5°C, εSAT=1°C, etc. Evidence: "5.22.6.5. The internal variables shown in Table 5.20.6.5 shall be defined..." This is parameter specification, which is part of operational knowledge (tuning). Accept. Parameter specs are valid types.

Eleventh: cand_0b1d9cecd418d8fb - fault_diagnostic_rule, title "FC#2: SAT Too Low in Full Heating", section 5.22.6.6. Summary: Fault condition when SAT_avg < SATsp - εSAT and HC ≥ 99% in OS#1. Evidence matches. Accept.

Twelfth: cand_c011398b677b9e6a - fault_diagnostic_rule, title "FC#3: SAT Too High in Full Cooling", section 5.22.6.6. Similar to above. Accept.

Thirteenth: cand_ac98fca736b73f9c - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Fault Condition Evaluation by OS", section 5.22.6.7. Summary: Subset evaluated depending on OS. Evidence: "5.22.6.7. A subset of all potential fault conditions is evaluated..." Accept.

Fourteenth: cand_bdc5097ac0b8b103 - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Alarm Suppression per FCU", section 5.22.6.8. Summary: Operator can suppress alarm for any fault condition per FCU. Evidence: "For each FCU, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition." Accept.

Fifteenth: cand_2e3a3856d1c05b9a - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Suspension Conditions", section 5.22.6.9. Summary: Suspended when FCU not operating, and for ModeDelay after mode change. Evidence matches. Accept.

Sixteenth: cand_85895492ad76bc78 - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Non-Applicable Fault Conditions", section 5.22.6.10. Summary: Fault conditions not applicable to current OS not evaluated. Evidence: "Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated." Accept.

Seventeenth: cand_b3a8db7c91d4ba34 - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Alarm Delay Requirement", section 5.22.6.11. Summary: Fault must persist continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before reporting. Evidence matches. Accept.

Eighteenth: cand_d4be5b2d165e8311 - operational_sequence, title "AFDD Test Mode", section 5.22.6.12. Summary: Test mode sets ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes. Evidence matches. Accept.

Nineteenth: cand_3f2ac651ebd5be63 - fault_diagnostic_rule, title "AFDD Fault Alarm Level and Content", section 5.22.6.13. Summary: When reported, Level 3 alarm with description and possible diagnoses from Table. Evidence matches. Accept.

Twentieth: cand_443c3937b6c16156 - operational_sequence, title "Chilled-Water Reset Requests Suppression", section 5.22.8.1.a. Summary: Suppress requests if fan not at MaxCoolSpeed. Evidence: "All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed." Accept.

Twenty-first: cand_60540e50f5a97ca4 - operational_sequence, title "Chilled-Water Reset Requests Logic", section 5.22.8.1.b-e. Summary: Step logic for sending 3,2,1,0 reset requests based on SAT deviation and valve position. Evidence matches. Accept.

Twenty-second: cand_6daf45702f0f86b1 - operational_sequence, title "Chiller Plant Request Logic", section 5.22.8.2. Summary: Send 1 chiller plant request if CHW valve >95% until <10%; else 0. Evidence matches. Accept.

Twenty-third: cand_9e848aea02dc07f0 - operational_sequence, title "Hot-Water Reset Requests Suppression", section 5.22.8.3.a. Summary: Suppress if fan not at MaxHeatSpeed. Evidence matches. Accept.

Twenty-fourth: cand_fe65fdeacc4a5a9b - operational_sequence, title "Hot-Water Reset Requests Logic", section 5.22.8.3.b-e. Summary: Step logic for hot-water reset requests. Evidence matches. Accept.

Twenty-fifth: cand_70cfc0399b61abf2 - operational_sequence, title "Heating Hot-Water Plant Request Logic", section 5.22.8.4. Summary: Send 1 heating plant request if HW valve >95% until <10%; else 0. Evidence matches. Accept.

All 26 candidates appear to be valid operational knowledge items directly from Guideline 36, with supporting evidence quotes. None are front matter, TOC, generic definitions, duplicates, or unsupported. So all should be accepted with is_valid=true and category_if_not=null.

Now output JSON array "verdicts" with each candidate_id.
